name: Clarifi - Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Delaware Valley
description_short: Non-profit financial and housing empowerment organization serving Greater Philadelphia for 50+ years, offering free certified counseling to low- and moderate-income residents.
description_long:
Clarifi, formerly known as Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Delaware Valley, was founded over 50 years ago and has grown into Greater Philadelphia's largest provider of financial and housing empowerment services. The organization serves low- and moderate-income residents across Southeastern Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and Delaware, operating on the belief that everyone deserves judgment-free financial resources tailored to their individual situation and goals.

Clarifi offers a comprehensive suite of services including certified financial empowerment counseling, housing success programs, benefits access assistance, and direct financial assistance. In 2024, the organization conducted 4,911 counseling sessions with 3,657 households, disbursed $1.3 million in grants, and secured $6.9 million for essential home repairs — reflecting a scope well beyond typical credit counseling agencies. Clients achieved an average debt reduction of $2,978.

What distinguishes Clarifi from most credit counseling nonprofits is its integration of housing services alongside financial counseling. The organization explicitly aims to help clients build intergenerational wealth, not just manage immediate crises, and pairs counseling with direct financial assistance such as home repair grants. Its stated 10,000+ annual clients and 5,100+ client milestones achieved in 2024 suggest meaningful throughput for a regional nonprofit.

Honestly, Clarifi is a strong regional resource but its reach is geographically limited to Southeastern PA, NJ, and Delaware. The website is light on specifics — fee structures, wait times, and eligibility thresholds are not disclosed publicly. The organization's focus on low-to-moderate income residents means higher-income consumers may not qualify. For those inside its service area and income bracket, it represents one of the more comprehensive free financial help options available.
pros: Founded 50+ years ago — one of the most established nonprofit counselors in the Philadelphia region
Largest financial and housing empowerment provider in Greater Philadelphia
Average client debt reduced by $2,978 per household based on 2024 data
$6.9 million secured for essential home repairs in 2024 — rare among credit counselors
$1.3 million in direct grants disbursed to clients in 2024
Certified financial empowerment counselors — judgment-free, tailored to individual situations
Serves three states: Southeastern PA, New Jersey, and Delaware
cons: Geographic restriction — only serves Southeastern PA, NJ, and Delaware; no national availability
Targeted at low-to-moderate income residents; higher-income consumers may not qualify
Website does not disclose specific eligibility criteria, fees, or typical wait times for counseling
Housing and repair programs likely have separate eligibility requirements not detailed on the site
best_for: Low-to-moderate income residents in Southeastern Pennsylvania, New Jersey, or Delaware
Homeowners needing affordable home repair assistance or housing counseling
Consumers in financial crisis seeking free, certified debt counseling
Individuals looking to build long-term financial stability, not just resolve immediate debt
diagnosis: Clarifi is best for low-to-moderate income residents in the Greater Philadelphia tri-state area (SE Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Delaware) who need free, certified financial or housing counseling with access to direct assistance programs. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation — consumers outside Southeastern PA, NJ, or Delaware will need to seek an alternative NFCC-member agency.
services: Home repair funding and referrals ($6.9M secured in 2024)
